calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-10-2011 Granted Per Written Order

Notice Of Attorneys Resignation; Petition For Reimbursement Of Expenses And Payment Of Attorneys Compensation
Off Calendar Apr-06-2011 Off Calendar

Petition Of Beneficiary Joan Giannini For Order Directing Personal Representative To Make A Preliminary Distribution Of Specific Bequest To Beneficiary
Off Calendar Nov-10-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurence Blau for Melissa Ruth (Executor). Haig Harris for Petitioner.

Mr. Blau spoke of the estate tax issues/debts, stated that they were trying to retrace the Decedents steps, that he had prepared a draft of the First and Final Account and that he was waiting for the taxes to be filed. In addition, Mr. Blau stated that there was $69,000.00 left in cash, that the car was worth $32,000.00 and that the stocks sold brought in $100,000.00. Mr. harris stated that he asked what the Administrator's plan was, that he never received an answer, that the Porsche was a depreciating asset, that they were paying for storage fees and that opposing Counsel could obtain stock statements that would provide the needed information. Mr. Blau asked for more time stating that the Executor knows that she has to sell the car. The Court granted the petition (order for the same signed in open Court).

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Anasse.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status And Setting Of The Will Contest, And For Service Of Notice Of The Will Contest
Off Calendar Jul-22-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Haig Harris for Joan Giannini (Contestant). Laurence Blau for Melissa Ruth.

Mr. Haig stated that there were 5 interested Parties, that the Executrix was personally served, that Colleen Carathan was personally served on June 3, 2010, that the Mother, Father and Sister of the Decedent were out of state and served by certified mail, and that he received the signed return receipts. In addition, he stated that there was an issue with the processing of the Request to Enter Default against the Father, and that there was an issue with the codicil as it leaves property that does not exist. Mr. Blau stated that the Executor has not taken a position on the will contest, that they would accept the Court's ruling, that they are not challenging substance and that they wanted to make sure proper procedure is followed. The Court stated that perhaps the proper way to proceed is to find that the Parties were served, that they did not respond and that the codicil is invalid. The matter was taken under submission. Mr. Haig will submit an order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status And Setting Of The Will Contest, And For Service Of Notice Of The Will Contest
Off Calendar Jun-09-2010 Continued To Jul-14-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Laurence Blau for Melissa Ruth. Haig Harris for Joan Giannini (Contestant).

Mr. Blau stated that the Executor is not going to oppose the contest of the codicil and that she would submit to whatever decision the Court renders on the matter. Mr. Haig stated that the contest was served on all Parties, that it was his assumption that no one was doing anything, that the Decedent's Sister was not a Beneficiary, that she had no interest and that the Estate was taking a neutral position. In addition, he stated that he could file a Request for Entry of Default 30 days from the date of June 3, 2010. The matter was continued to July 14, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of any response/opposition to the codicil contest and for status of the Request for Entry of Default.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-16-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Haig Harris for Joan Giannini (Beneficiary/Contestant). Laurence Blau for Melissa Ruth.

Mr. Harris stated that he filed the will contest last week, that he had no opposition to the petition to admit the will into probate as it was appropriately witnessed but that he was contesting the Codicil as it lacked formality to be valid and stated that the Decedent lacked capacity. Mr. Blau stated that he received a copy of the will contest, procedurally requested that the will be accepted and stated that they would deal with the Codicil at a later date. The Court granted the petition (subject to the order being modified to exclude the codicil). Bond is fixed at $190,000.00. Mr. Harris requested that service of the will contest be deferred. Status and setting of the will contest and service of notice of the will contest was set for June 9, 2010 at 9:00 a.m, in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
